title: Staining Sequenza
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2q260","text":"This protocol describes the use of sequenza staining device. The sequenza can be use for MIBI or IHC staining methods.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"f0ike","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b03ru","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"3a70n","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"tables","mutability":"","data":{"cellsMeta":{},"colTitles":["","",""],"colWidths":["196","209","193"],"data":[["Product","Provider","Cat.Number"],["Thermo Scientific Shandon Glass Coverplates","Fisher Scientific","72-110-017 "],["Thermo Scientific Shandon Sequenza Immunostaining Center Accessories, slide rack","Fisher Scientific","73-310-017"]],"guid":"FA211440930A11EBB771DFA79D6A0C45","isJexcelDataFormat":true,"legend":"","mergeCells":{},"printData":[["Product","Provider","Cat.Number"],["Thermo Scientific Shandon Glass Coverplates","Fisher Scientific","72-110-017 "],["Thermo Scientific Shandon Sequenza Immunostaining Center Accessories, slide rack","Fisher Scientific","73-310-017"]],"rowHeights":[23,23,23]}}}}
steps: Fill a disposable Pipetting Reservoir with 20 mL of 1x PBS wash buffer
Place sample slide on a Sequenza cover plate aligning the bottom slide with the notches on the cover plate (see picture below panel A)
Fill by capillarity the space between the slide and cover plate by holding the parts tight and dipping the bottom part of the assembly in the wash buffer reservoir (see picture above panel B)
Transfer the slide and coverplate assembly into the Sequenza rack. Slip in the assembly (see below picture panel C)
Secure the assembly and make sure that the assembly placed down in the rack (see above picture panel D)
Add 200 µL of Blocking buffer)
Place the cover and incubate with the blocking buffer for 1h
Add 1 mL of wash buffer. The buffer should flow thru within 1 min 30 s. Repeat by adding 1 mL of wash buffer
If you are using biotinylated antibody or probe, you need to block endogenous biotin Add 3 drops of Avidin solution sufficient to cover the sample and incubate for 00:10:00 Room temperature
Add 1 mL of wash buffer
Add drops Biotin solution sufficient to cover the sample and incubate for 00:10:00 Room temperature
Add 1 mL of wash buffer
Next day, use anti-biotin metal-labeled antibody (1D4-C5) in Stain 2 panel
If presence of bubbles are observed after adding Avidin and Biotin solution. Dismount the slide and the Sequenza cover plate
Fill by capillarity the space between the slide and cover plate by holding the parts tight and dipping the bottom part of the assembly in the wash buffer reservoir (see picture above panel B)
Spin down briefly all antibody tubes at 10.000 x g, °C, for 00:01:00 , preferably at 4 °C
Pipet the respective amount of each individual antibody. Do not disturb the bottom of the antibody tube when pipetting
The total volume needed for staining each Sequenza slide assembly is 120 µL The manufacturer recommend to use 100 µL per Sequenza slide assembly. 120 µL is therefore a 20% excess.
Prepare 0.1 µm filter unit

Prepare a panel table in excel including the following information: Conjugation ID, Target name, Channel, , Antibody concentration, Titer, Volume Exemple: A B C D E F 1 ID Target name Channel Concentration (ug/mL) Titer (ug/mL) Calculation formulation for Volume (µL) 2 1299 CD4 143 50 1 3 3 1221 CD11c 161 50 0.25 0.75 4 1292 CD3 159 50 0.5 1.5 5 ... ... ... ... ... 6 Total volume (µL) 150 7 Antibody diluent volume 144.75 8 Antibody mix volume 5.25
Add first, antibody diluent (NHS 3%) to complete to the total volume
Add 400 µL of antibody diluent buffer (NHS 3%) to a 0.1 µm centrifugal filter device
Spin at 10,000 x g for 1 min
Discard the flowthough by aspirating
Add the antibody mix to the filter unit
Spin at 10,000 x g for 1 min
After, blocking incubation, add 200 µL of antibody buffer (NHS 3%)
Make sure that the antibody buffer has passed through to prevent dilution of antibody mix. Note: this should take no more than 2 min
Add antibody master mix, 120 µL The manufacturer recommend to use 100 µL per Sequenza slide assembly. 120 µL is therefore a 20% excess.
Place back the rack cover, transfer to 4˚C refrigerator, and incubate overnight
Prepare 1 hr antibody master mix as described above in steps 15 to 18 Optional: include use anti-biotin or other anti-hapten antibodies when apply
Following overnight incubation, wash with 1 mL of wash buffer
Repeat wash by 1 mL of wash buffer
Add 300 µL antibody buffer (NHS 3%)
Make sure that the antibody buffer has passed thru to prevent dilution of the antibody mix
Add Stain 2 antibody mix and incubate for 1 hour at 4˚C
The slide preparation is the same as described in the protocol MIBI staining dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bh9zj976
Add 1 mL of wash buffer
Repeat step 29
Remove the slide for the assembly
Immerse the slide in post-fixation buffer (glutaraldehyde 4% in PBS-low Barium) for 5 min
Transfer in PBS-low Barium for rinse, <2 min
Rearrange and fill additional reagent containers: Tris buffer pH 8.5 x 3, ddH2O x 2, 70% EtOH, 80% EtOH, 95% EtOH x 2, 100% EtOH x 2, exit tank = empty and dry
Insert slides into slide carriers. Place slide carriers into placeholder containers into the first Tris buffer container
Menu --> Processing time = 30 sec, Lift bar = 976, Number of dips = 3, Start position = position of the first slide carrier
Press Run
Dry in vacuum desiccator with the slide carrier lid open for at least 1 hour prior to MIBI analysis
creator: Marc Bosse
